behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS is low: this behavior is rule math/lookup logic inside the scoring model. No real OS/network/subprocess is required (REAL-INFRA no), no AI-output dependence (LLM-DEP no), and no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejected Integration because the behavior text is limited to 'assigns the correct risk score' for one threshold condition, which is fully testable at a local function/object boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Add a table-driven unit test for scoring logic with merchant-category threshold configs and transactions just below/at/above threshold; assert exact numeric risk score deltas and deterministic handling of category-specific config.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test stays within a pure scoring boundary and does not rely on cross-module infrastructure.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the real model’s fraud judgment quality ('identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern') rather than deterministic plumbing. A stub would mask model-decision failures (LLM-DEP yes by policy for real AI/ML model calls). Not Workflow because this is a single model decision, not multiple agentic steps with cross-step dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk model on a labeled eval slice containing pattern-based fraud cases where rule thresholds are individually below limits; assert fraud probability/ranking separates fraud vs non-fraud at an agreed operating point, with repeated runs tolerance bands if model output is slightly variable.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test explicitly requires real model inference quality, which matches Agentic.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: This can fail at module handoff boundaries (time-window config parsing, transaction retrieval, per-user filtering, clock/window logic), so unit-only can give false confidence (MOCK-MISS yes). No explicit requirement for real OS/network/subprocess or external contract (REAL-INFRA no), and no dependence on AI model output (LLM-DEP no). Rejected System because behavior text does not require real external boundary validation.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring scoring service + transaction repository (local DB/fixture store) with seeded timestamped transactions across users and boundary times; assert per-user counts within/outside window and resulting velocity contribution to score.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires multi-module state/query interaction but not real external infrastructure.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is orchestration/aggregation logic across model outputs (MOCK-MISS yes). This behavior can be validated by wiring decision modules and asserting hold-state propagation. Rejected Agentic because the text fixes the input condition ('flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model'), so deterministic model-output fixtures are sufficient; it tests gate logic, not model quality. Rejected System because no explicit external contract/round-trip requirement is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with scoring output forced to not-flag and risk output forced to flag; execute decision pipeline and assert transaction status is 'held for review' and AND-style regression would fail.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test exercises cross-module decision wiring, matching Integration.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily contract composition across internal modules (score + probability + flag provenance), which unit-only may miss when serializers/assemblers are wired together (MOCK-MISS yes). Rejected System because behavior text does not explicitly require real HTTP/UI/export round-trip verification of external contract; it only states combined output content. Rejected Agentic because presence/shape of fields can be tested with deterministic outputs and does not require judging model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test through the output-assembly path: provide known scoring and risk outputs, execute combiner/response builder, and assert payload contains numeric score, fraud probability, per-model flag indicators, and explanatory fields used by reviewers.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires multi-module output assembly but no real external infrastructure.
